title: Recover the Heap After Duplicate Element Removal

statement.markdown: Given a max heap `nums` of integers where each integer may occur more than once, remove all duplicate values while preserving the max heap property. Return the modified heap.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers representing a max heap.
statement.outputFormat: A modified max heap array of integers with all duplicates removed.
statement.notes: A max heap satisfies the max heap property: for every node `i`, the value of `i` is greater than or equal to the values of its children.

examples.explanation: Remove duplicate 3, preserving max heap property: [3, 5, 5, 5] -> [3, 5, 5, 2]

hints.text: Use a set to track unique elements and maintain the max heap property during removals.

editorial.title: Correcting the Recovery Process for Max Heap with Duplicate Elements
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use a set to store unique elements and then insert them back into the max heap. This ensures the removal of duplicates while maintaining the max heap property.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Rebuild the max heap from scratch without duplicates, resulting in O(n log n) time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: Maintain a set of unique elements and use a priority queue to efficiently insert and remove duplicates.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a set to store unique elements and maintain the max heap property during removals, achieving O(n log k) time complexity, where k is the number of unique elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This solution correctly maintains the max heap property by using a set to remove duplicates and a priority queue for efficient insertions and removals.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run using a sample input.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly using a non-priority data structure, resulting in incorrect max heap property.
